opp.name: ABB_Intake+ANA_Jun'26
opp.next_step: Follow up to get NDA/mNDA for legal review. Provide ABB with Zycus security/data-isolation documentation. Schedule a deep-dive discovery session with a Solution Consultant focused on ANA (autonomous negotiation) and Merlin Intake, tailored around Infor LX integration questions.
opp.ais_why:
• ABB is 'exploring AI-driven procurement transformation, currently running ad hoc AI pilots without a structured framework' ‚Äî the buyer is already experimenting with AI and seeking a structured approach.
• Stated objective is to 'automate maverick/tail spend negotiation first,' aligning directly with Autonomous Negotiation; ANA, Agentic AI and Merlin Intake are all scoped on the opportunity.
• Compelling event is framed as ABB 'seeking a structured approach to AI in supply chain,' making AI the explicit driver rather than a side feature.

sources: Avoma discovery call 12 Jun 2026, Dillan Patel (Data Analytics Manager): described manual procurement workflows, lack of AI automation, need for intelligent intake and negotiation agents
SF Customer_Business_Problem__c and Next_Step__c 30 Jun: reference ANA (autonomous negotiation) and Merlin Intake scope but no quantified metrics
metrics.narrative: ABB articulated a qualitative business problem on the 12 Jun 2026 discovery call: procurement inefficiency and lack of AI-driven automation in supplier intake and negotiation workflows. Dillan Patel (Data Analytics Manager) described their current state as manual, fragmented across systems, and lacking intelligent agent-based capabilities for contract negotiation and intake triage. However, no quantified impact was stated—no cycle-time reduction target, cost savings goal, headcount efficiency gain, or error-rate improvement. The pain is confirmed but not monetized. The Metrics element remains partial until a quantified business case is developed, ideally in partnership with the Economic Buyer or a finance-aligned sponsor. Recommended move: run a value-discovery workshop with ABB procurement leadership to quantify manual process costs, cycle time, and ROI potential for Merlin Intake and ANA deployment.

sources: Avoma discovery call 12 Jun 2026: Dillan Patel (Data Analytics Manager) attended, expressed interest in Merlin Intake and ANA, described procurement pain
Avoma follow-up call 18 Jun 2026: Dillan attended, deep-dive on AI capabilities, provided technical requirements
Salesforce contact role: Dillan Patel listed, no other stakeholders
SF Next_Step__c 30 Jun: references scheduling deep-dive demo with Dillan as primary contact
champion.narrative: Dillan Patel (Data Analytics Manager) is the only engaged stakeholder and is behaving as a technical advocate and coach, not yet a champion. He initiated the engagement, attended two discovery calls (12 Jun, 18 Jun), expressed genuine interest in Merlin Intake and ANA, and provided detailed technical requirements (Infor LX integration, security, AI maturity). He is actively engaged (last contact 18 Jun, referenced in Next_Step 30 Jun) and has not gone dark. However, Dillan has provided no evidence of upward access to the Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or procurement leadership. He has not introduced Zycus to senior stakeholders, has not shared internal business-case documents, has not pushed back on Coupa (the named competitor) on Zycus's behalf, and has not driven the agenda beyond technical discovery. His role is technical liaison and gatekeeper, not commercial champion. Champion strength: developing. What has developed: Dillan is engaged, responsive, and technically aligned with the relevant stakeholder capabilities. What is missing: access to decision-makers, internal advocacy at the executive or procurement-leadership level, and evidence that he is selling Zycus internally. Risk: without upward access, this remains a technical pilot interest that may never reach commercial evaluation. Recommended move: on the next call, ask Dillan directly who owns the procurement AI strategy and budget at ABB, and request an introduction to that leader for a value-alignment conversation. Build Dillan into a coach by asking him to map the internal stakeholders and decision process, then use that map to gain multi-threaded access.

sources: SF Competitors__c: Coupa
Avoma discovery call 12 Jun 2026: no mention of Coupa
Avoma follow-up call 18 Jun 2026: no mention of Coupa
SF Next_Step__c, Next_Step_History__c, Tasks: no Coupa references
competition.narrative: Coupa is named as a competitor in Salesforce Competitors__c, but no competitive intelligence has been gathered across any call, task, or free-text field. Neither the 12 Jun nor 18 Jun Avoma call transcripts mention Coupa, and no Next_Step, Task, or Salesforce field describes ABB's relationship with Coupa (incumbent vs peer evaluation), Coupa's positioning, or ABB's sentiment toward Coupa. It is unknown whether Coupa is the entrenched incumbent (high displacement risk) or a shortlisted peer in a greenfield evaluation (neutral competitive context). No competitive wedge or differentiation strategy has been articulated. What the reconciled read shows: Coupa is a known competitor, but the competitive dynamics are opaque. Zycus has not yet positioned against Coupa or validated whether ABB is dissatisfied with Coupa or simply exploring alternatives. Recommended move: on the next call, ask Dillan directly how ABB is currently handling intake and negotiation (incumbent tooling, manual process, or Coupa), what they like and dislike about their current approach, and whether Coupa is in the evaluation. Then position Zycus's Merlin Intake and ANA agentic AI capabilities as differentiation against Coupa's workflow-automation model—Zycus offers autonomous agents that negotiate and triage, not just workflow orchestration. The wedge: Merlin/ANA autonomous intelligence vs Coupa's static workflow tools. Activation: make the deep-dive demo a head-to-head Merlin vs Coupa capability comparison on a live ABB use case (e.g., supplier pricing negotiation or intake triage), and ask Dillan to score both on his stated criteria (AI maturity, speed to value, ease of configuration).

sources: Avoma discovery call 12 Jun 2026, Dillan Patel: 'manual, time-intensive, fragmented intake and negotiation processes, no intelligent automation'
Avoma follow-up call 18 Jun 2026, Dillan Patel: 'speed to value and ease of configuration critical, cannot afford long disruptive implementation'
SF Customer_Business_Problem__c: references procurement inefficiency and lack of AI automation
identify_pain.narrative: ABB's procurement pain is confirmed and clearly articulated across two discovery calls. On 12 Jun 2026, Dillan Patel (Data Analytics Manager) described their current supplier intake and contract negotiation processes as 'manual, time-intensive, and fragmented across multiple systems with no intelligent automation.' He stated that ABB procurement teams spend significant effort on repetitive intake triage and contract redlining that could be automated, and that they lack AI-driven negotiation agents to handle supplier pricing and terms optimization at scale. On 18 Jun, Dillan added that 'speed to value and ease of configuration are critical because we cannot afford a long implementation that disrupts current workflows.' The pain is operational inefficiency and lack of AI-powered procurement intelligence, owned by ABB's procurement organization (Dillan is the technical liaison, but the pain sits with procurement leadership). The pain is confirmed but not quantified—no cycle-time, cost, or headcount impact stated. Inferred pain: competitive pressure to modernize procurement with AI (Dillan referenced 'AI maturity' as an evaluation criterion, implying ABB sees AI adoption as strategic). Recommended move: validate and quantify this pain with ABB procurement leadership in a value-discovery session.

sources: Avoma discovery call 12 Jun 2026, Dillan Patel: NDA required, security/data-isolation flagged as top concerns and formal gate
SF Next_Step__c 30 Jun: NDA/mNDA for legal review, security documentation delivery both listed as open actions
SF Current_Contract_Expiration__c blank
paper_process.narrative: Two contracting and governance requirements have surfaced but are unresolved. First, ABB legal must review and approve an NDA or mNDA before deeper engagement—this was stated on the 12 Jun call and carried forward in Next_Step 30 Jun as an open Zycus deliverable ('Follow up to get NDA/mNDA for legal review'). Second, security and data-isolation documentation must be provided and cleared by ABB's InfoSec team before advancing to formal evaluation or demo, also flagged 12 Jun and reiterated 30 Jun. No MSA, SOW, or procurement-channel mechanics have been discussed. No SI, partner, or implementation scoping has occurred. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is blank, so no incumbent-contract forcing function is visible. What is known: ABB has a formal legal and InfoSec gate that must clear before technical deep-dive or commercial conversation. What is unknown: procurement channel (direct vs partner), MSA/SOW process, legal redline expectations, and whether an SI will be involved. Recommended move: provide the NDA and security documentation immediately (both are open commitments), and once cleared, ask Dillan or ABB legal to map the full contracting and procurement process for a deal of this size and technical scope.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified or engaged. Salesforce contact roles show no EB, Executive Sponsor, or budget-holding procurement leader. The 12 Jun and 18 Jun Avoma calls involved only Dillan Patel (Data Analytics Manager), a technical advocate with no commercial or budget authority. No pricing, budget, or approval-process conversation has occurred across any call, task, or Salesforce field. SF fields Who_will_approve_budget__c, X10a_Sponsor__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, and Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c are all blank. The deal is five days old and early-stage (Qualified), so EB absence is not immediately fatal, but without upward access this deal risks stalling at pilot-interest stage. What the evidence shows: Dillan is a technical gatekeeper and advocate (he initiated contact, expressed interest in AI agents, and engaged twice), but he cannot approve budget. Recommended move: ask Dillan directly on the next call to map the procurement budget owner and decision chain, and request an introduction to the VP Procurement or CFO-aligned sponsor for a value-alignment conversation.

decision_process.narrative: No formal decision process has been mapped. The call sequence shows only Dillan Patel (Data Analytics Manager) across two discovery calls (12 Jun, 18 Jun), with no procurement, legal, IT, or executive participation. No senior stakeholder has joined, no evaluation stage has been formalized, and no approval chain has been discussed. Salesforce fields What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, and Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c are blank. The engagement is purely exploratory and technical at this stage—Dillan is researching AI capabilities, not running a formal vendor evaluation. What the evidence shows: this is a bottoms-up, technical-advocate-led discovery, not a top-down procurement process. The risk is that Dillan lacks authority to move this forward without a formal business case and executive sponsorship. Recommended move: on the next call, ask Dillan to describe ABB's typical procurement decision process for enterprise software (stages, stakeholders, approval gates, timeline), and request a meeting with his manager or the procurement lead to understand how this would proceed if ABB decides to advance.

sources: Avoma discovery call 12 Jun 2026, Dillan Patel: AI automation, Infor LX integration, security/data isolation as top concerns
Avoma follow-up call 18 Jun 2026, Dillan Patel: ease of configuration, speed to value, AI maturity
SF Next_Step__c 30 Jun: Infor LX integration questions flagged for deep-dive demo
SF Business_Requirements__c: references AI needs, intake automation, negotiation agent use cases
decision_criteria.narrative: ABB has stated partial evaluation criteria across two discovery calls. On 12 Jun 2026, Dillan Patel identified three key requirements: (1) AI-driven automation for supplier intake and contract negotiation (confirmed interest in Merlin Intake and ANA), (2) seamless integration with their Infor LX ERP system (stated as non-negotiable, referenced again in Next_Step 30 Jun), and (3) enterprise-grade security and data isolation, described as 'top concerns' and a formal gating requirement before deeper engagement. On 18 Jun, Dillan added that ABB is evaluating 'ease of configuration, speed to value, and vendor AI maturity' but did not provide a weighted scorecard or formal RFP criteria. The criteria are confirmed but incomplete—no formal RFP, no decision rubric, no procurement-led structured evaluation process visible yet. What is missing: multi-stakeholder criteria (procurement, legal, IT, finance perspectives), competitive evaluation framework, and formal decision timeline. This is a technical-led early discovery, not yet a structured vendor selection. Recommended move: request a stakeholder workshop with procurement, IT, and legal to align on weighted decision criteria and evaluation process before advancing to formal demo or proposal.

items.action: Deliver the open commitments immediately: send the Zycus NDA/mNDA to Dillan Patel for ABB legal review, and provide the security/data-isolation documentation (closed-loop access controls, data-protection architecture) to clear the InfoSec gate. Both are formal blockers flagged 12 Jun and still open as of 30 Jun Next_Step. Clearing these unblocks the deep-dive demo and formal evaluation.
items.trigger: Security and NDA flagged as top concerns and formal gates on 12 Jun call; both listed as open actions in Next_Step 30 Jun.
items.expected_effect: Clears legal and InfoSec blockers, enables scheduling of deep-dive demo and progression to formal evaluation.

items.action: On the next call with Dillan Patel, ask him to map ABB's procurement budget owner and decision chain for enterprise software purchases of this scope, and request an introduction to the VP Procurement or CFO-aligned sponsor for a value-alignment and ROI conversation. items.trigger: No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or procurement leadership engaged across any call, task, or Salesforce contact role. Dillan Patel is a technical advocate with no budget authority. Single-threaded risk identified 12 Jun and persists.
items.expected_effect: Gains upward access to budget holder and procurement leadership, multi-threads the deal, and de-risks single-threaded stall at pilot stage. Enables commercial and approval-process mapping.

items.action: Schedule and deliver the deep-dive demo with a Solution Consultant focused on Merlin Intake and ANA, tailored around ABB's Infor LX ERP integration questions. Structure the demo as a head-to-head capability comparison: Zycus autonomous agents (Merlin negotiation agent on supplier pricing, ANA intake triage) vs Coupa's workflow-automation model, scored by Dillan on his stated criteria (AI maturity, speed to value, ease of configuration). Use a live ABB procurement use case (supplier pricing negotiation or intake triage) to demonstrate differentiation. Invite Dillan's manager or a procurement-side stakeholder to the demo to begin multi-threading.

items.action: On the next call with Dillan Patel, ask directly how ABB is currently handling supplier intake and contract negotiation (manual process, incumbent tooling, or Coupa), what they like and dislike about the current approach, and whether Coupa is in this evaluation. deltas.to: Dillan Patel (Data Analytics Manager) - developing
deltas.subject: champion
Demonstrate Merlin Intake and Anna in a deep-dive session tailored to the relevant stakeholder integration questions
Marco Talassio
Christy Lingenfelter
Nikhil Kapur

Multi-thread upward to procurement or finance leadership (VP Procurement, Head of Indirect Procurement, or CFO) to map budget authority and decision process. Dillan Patel is an engaged technical advocate but not a power role; without upward access the deal remains single-threaded and vulnerable to stalling at the pilot stage.

Quantify the tail-spend and maverick-purchase opportunity during the deep-dive demo. ABB articulated the pain (manual, fragmented processes) but no ROI, cost savings, or cycle-time reduction has been discussed. Build the business case with Dillan and push it upward to justify budget.

Clarify Coupa's status and role in the competitive landscape. Coupa is named in SF but was not mentioned on the discovery call. Determine whether Coupa is the incumbent being replaced, a parallel evaluation, or a do-nothing alternative, then sharpen the agentic-AI wedge (Anna, Merlin Intake) Coupa cannot match.
Add buyer-side contact roles to the opportunity. Zero contact roles are logged; this is a data-hygiene gap that weakens stakeholder visibility and multi-thread tracking.
